name: Martinsville First Savings Bank
description_short: Martinsville First Savings Bank is a mutual savings bank founded in 1924 offering mortgages, certificates of deposit, checking, savings, and mobile banking to southwest Virginia communities.
description_long:
Martinsville First Savings Bank was established in 1924 by local businessmen who identified a need for mortgage lending to support the growing city of Martinsville. The bank has operated continuously for nearly 100 years as a community financial institution, positioning itself as a cornerstone of Martinsville and surrounding areas in southwest Virginia.

The bank offers a range of retail banking services including mortgages (currently featuring 30-year fixed-rate mortgages at 5.99% APR), certificates of deposit with varying terms and rates (ranging from 3.14% to 3.53% APY), interest-bearing checking and savings accounts, online and mobile banking platforms, tax form access, and loan products. They actively promote their CD offerings as a savings vehicle paired with interest-bearing accounts.

As a mutual bank, Martinsville First Savings Bank is owned by its depositors rather than shareholders, which the institution emphasizes as a key differentiator. This structure means reinvested deposits stay local, and the bank prioritizes community development and support of local organizations, businesses, and initiatives. The mutual ownership model is central to their brand messaging about the "mutual bank difference."

The bank appears well-established with proper regulatory credentials (NMLS# 686254, Routing# 251472759) and offers modern conveniences like mobile banking apps available on both iOS and Android. However, as a regional community bank, their product offerings and rates are more limited compared to larger national institutions, and specific details about account minimums, fees, and full service availability are not disclosed on the provided website content. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of deposit with multiple terms (7-month, 13-month, 27-month, 39-month)
Online banking account enrollment and access
pros: Mutual bank structure means deposits are reinvested locally in southwest Virginia communities rather than distributed to shareholders
Mobile banking app available on both iOS and Android for convenient account access
Competitive CD rates with multiple term options (7-month at 3.49% APY, 13-month at 3.34%, 27-month at 3.29%, 39-month at 3.14%)
Nearly 100-year operating history since 1924 demonstrates stability and community integration
30-year fixed mortgage rate of 5.99% APR competitively positioned
Online account enrollment and login system for digital banking convenience
Straightforward tax form access process for eStatement and paper statement holders
cons: Limited geographic availability as a regional community bank serving only southwest Virginia
Website does not disclose account minimum balances, monthly fees, or overdraft policies
No information provided about other banking products such as auto loans, personal loans, or lines of credit
Regional institution may have fewer ATM locations compared to national banks
Limited details on savings account rates and terms available
best_for: Southwest Virginia residents seeking community-focused banking with local reinvestment of deposits
Homebuyers in the Martinsville area looking for fixed-rate mortgages with straightforward application processes
Savers interested in certificate of deposit products with competitive rates tied to community banking
diagnosis: Martinsville First Savings Bank is best suited for southwest Virginia residents who prioritize community banking and want their deposits reinvested locally, particularly those seeking mortgages or high-yield CDs. The main caveat is that this is a regional community bank with limited geographic footprint and product disclosures, so customers should verify specific account terms, fees, and service availability before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
